parameters.toolDescription:
=Use this tool to look up a person's contact details in the Contacts Google Sheet.
Use it when the user mentions a person and you need their email or phone number.

Input:
contact_name: name of the person.

Returns:
Name, Email, and Phone.

values.lookupValue: ={{ $fromAI("contact_name", "Name of the person to search in the contacts sheet") }}

parameters.toolDescription:
Send a message iUse this tool to send a confirmation or reminder email after scheduling a meeting.

Inputs:
attendee_email - email address of the attendee
subject - subject of the email
message_body - email body text

Rules:
- Always send the email to both the attendee and me.
- My email address is maths.ace.it@gmail.com.
- Send the email only after the calendar event has been created successfully.
parameters.subject: ={{ $fromAI("subject", "Subject of the email") }}

parameters.toolDescription:
This tool MUST be used to create the event in Google Calendar when the user asks to schedule or create a meeting or event.

Required inputs:
title – title of the event
start_time – start date and time in ISO format with timezone Asia/Karachi (UTC+05:00) (example: 2024-12-25T14:00:00)
end_time – end date and time in ISO format with timezone Asia/Karachi (UTC+05:00)

Rules:
- If the user provides a duration (for example "1 hour", "30 minutes"), calculate the end_time accordingly.
- If the user provides both start and end time, use them exactly.
- If the user provides only a start time and no duration, assume a 1 hour meeting.

Return confirmation after the event is created.
parameters.start: ={{ $fromAI("start_time", "Start datetime in ISO format with timezone Asia/Karachi (UTC+05:00) like 2026-03-14T15:00:00+05:00") }}
parameters.end: ={{ $fromAI("end_time", "End datetime in ISO format with timezone Asia/Karachi (UTC+05:00) like 2026-03-14T16:00:00+05:00") }}

attendees: ={{ $fromAI("attendee_email", "Email address of the attendee to invite") }}

options.systemMessage:
=You are a helpful assistant connected to tools.

Rules:
- Use the contacts tool if the user asks for an email or phone number.

- When the user asks to schedule an event, you MUST call the calendar tool and create a real event in Google Calendar.

- Inputs required:
title – title of the event
start_time – start datetime in ISO format like 2026-03-15T15:00:00
end_time – end datetime in ISO format like 2026-03-15T16:00:00

- Do not respond that the meeting is scheduled unless the tool has been executed successfully.
- After receiving the tool results, answer the user immediately.
- Do not call the same tool repeatedly.
- If the tool returns data, extract the answer and respond.
- If no results are found, say the contact was not found.

Always finish with a final answer.
